summary: The CI job failed because a wget command in the Dockerfile received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error when attempting to download the nydus binary from GitHub releases. This caused the Docker build step to fail with exit code 1, which propagated as a buildx bake failure.
root_cause: A transient HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub's servers caused the wget download of the nydus static binary (https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz) to fail during the Docker build step.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: This is the direct error from wget showing a transient network/server error from GitHub when downloading the nydus binary.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This confirms the Docker build step failed due to the wget command returning a non-zero exit code.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the final GitHub Actions error annotation confirming the buildx bake command failed due to the network error.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job as this is a transient network failure (HTTP 502 from GitHub). Alternatively, consider adding retry logic to the wget command in the Dockerfile (e.g., using `wget --tries=3`) or using a mirror/cache for the nydus binary download.

summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because GitHub's servers returned repeated 502 errors when pip attempted to download a package from `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with the same network error.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated HTTP 502 errors when pip tried to fetch `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`, causing `HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded` on all 4 attempts (1 initial + 3 reruns).

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This error message appears in the captured stderr for all 4 runs of the test, confirming that GitHub returned 502 errors every time pip tried to download the package.
The test was rerun 3 times (as shown by 3 RERUN entries), indicating the test framework detected a flaky failure and retried, but all attempts failed with the same network error.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, confirming this is an isolated network failure rather than a code regression.

suggested_fix: This is a transient GitHub infrastructure issue (502 errors). No code change is needed; re-running the CI job should resolve the failure. Consider adding retry logic or marking this test with a network-flaky marker to auto-skip or retry on 5xx errors.

summary: A Jest test in `test/config/get.ts` failed because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected no trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This caused 1 test suite to fail and the overall CI job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command now returns registry URLs with a trailing slash, but the test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expects the URL without a trailing slash. The assertion `expect(stdout.toString().trim()).toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com')` fails because the actual output is `'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'`.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: This is the exact assertion mismatch — the received value has a trailing slash that the test does not expect.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test suite and one test failed.
Points to the exact line in the test file where the assertion fails.

suggested_fix: Update the test expectation at `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash (`.toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`) or, if the trailing slash is undesired behavior, fix the `pnpm config get` command to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before outputting them.
